headlines.title: Belgium’s Doku welcomes son during World Cup amid commentator controversy
headlines.snippet: Belgian footballer Doku welcomed a son during the World Cup. This occurred amid controversy surrounding a French presenter who was stood down after stating fathers are 'useless' at childbirth.

headlines.title: Egypt denied flight to Seattle ahead of final World Cup group match
headlines.snippet: Egypt's national football team was denied a flight to Seattle ahead of their final World Cup group match. The team will now return to their training base in Spokane.

headlines.title: US House committee reaches bipartisan deal on social media rules for kids
headlines.snippet: A US House committee has reached a bipartisan agreement on rules for social media use by children. While specific details were not released, leaders stated the legislation aims to hold 'Big Tech accountable'.

headlines.title: ‘Stop genocide’: Activist stopped by police at Tokyo’s Israeli Embassy
headlines.snippet: An activist in Tokyo was surrounded by police after approaching the Israeli Embassy and shouting slogans. The activist's protest included the call to 'Stop genocide'.

headlines.title: Hungary’s PM launches drive to free country from Orban’s ‘mafia’
headlines.snippet: Hungary's Prime Minister has initiated a campaign to free the country from what he describes as Orban's 'mafia'. Proposed changes include a new constitution, an anti-corruption office, and the removal of the president.

headlines.title: IMF warns Middle East instability could have lasting impact on Africa
headlines.snippet: The IMF warns that Middle East instability, exacerbated by the US-Israeli war on Iran, could have a lasting negative impact on Africa. Disruptions to fertilizer exports may significantly affect Africa's food security and agricultural costs, according to the IMF's Africa director, Zeine Zeidane.

headlines.title: Vance says Iran must back commitments with actions, not words
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance stated that progress in talks with Iran will be judged by actions, not words, despite calling recent negotiations productive. He emphasized that Iran allowing weapons and nuclear inspectors into the country is significant, but the US will monitor the inspectors' access.

headlines.title: Gaza death toll rises to 73,035 as Israeli attacks continue
headlines.snippet: Gaza's death toll has reached 73,035, with 173,368 Palestinians wounded since October 2023, according to medical sources cited by Wafa. The figures also show 1,005 Palestinians killed since the October ceasefire, indicating continued Israeli attacks.

headlines.title: US suspends Iran sanctions after 'good progress' in talks
headlines.snippet: The US has suspended sanctions against Iran following productive high-level talks in Switzerland. However, disagreements over nuclear and weapons inspections by both sides could still complicate the progress made during the negotiations.

headlines.title: Vance says US and Iranian negotiators remain in Switzerland for talks
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance confirmed that American and Iranian negotiators are continuing discussions in Switzerland. Vance departed, but left a team to work on the details of the interim agreement ahead of the planned signing.

headlines.title: Trump Says Strait of Hormuz Is Open
headlines.snippet: Donald Trump stated that the Strait of Hormuz is open.

headlines.snippet: The article discusses the potential damage to Keir Starmer's legacy, suggesting that his government's support for Israel's actions in Gaza and its stance on migrants will have lasting effects.

headlines.title: Marwan Barghouti and the World Cup
headlines.snippet: The text describes Marwan Barghouti as an exceptional football fan who transforms into a childlike and mischievous person while watching the World Cup.

headlines.title: Petro says Israel helped rig Colombia's presidential vote
headlines.snippet: Colombian President Petro suggested that Israel may have 'rigged' the country's presidential election, accusing them of tampering with the National Registry.

headlines.snippet: While the US and Iran differ on the status of the Strait of Hormuz, falling traffic data suggests Tehran still has the capability to significantly disrupt shipping.

headlines.snippet: Experts believe that trials related to the Assad-era in Syria are a significant step for accountability, but emphasize the need for fair and transparent proceedings.
